title: quercus × oviedoensis
intro: Quercus × oviedoensis, a hybrid oak species, is a rare plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high levels of tannins and flavonoids. The plant's extracts have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Quercus × oviedoensis is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
